author: codybontecou
description: This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff. Render Leaflet maps inline from iso.me exports. Supports JSON, CSV, Markdown, OwnTracks, Overland, and GPX formats with stats and duration-encoded markers.

manifest.author: Cody Bontecou
manifest.description: Render Leaflet maps inline from iso.me exports. Supports JSON, CSV, Markdown, OwnTracks, Overland, and GPX formats with stats and duration-encoded markers.
manifest.name: iso.me Maps

name: iso.me Maps

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's Changed

- Added iso.me v1.5 nested export folder support with flat, year, month, week, day, and custom folder structures.
- Added support for iso.me filename/path template tokens such as {year}, {month}, {day}, {type}, {format}, and {datetime}.
- Date keyword lookups now fall back to flat export paths for backwards compatibility during migrations.
- Folder sources and globs can load configured nested layouts, including explicit recursive ** globs.
- Includes the auto-fit map viewport option from the latest main branch.

## Installation

Download main.js, manifest.json, and styles.css into your vault's .obsidian/plugins/iso-me-maps folder, then reload Obsidian.

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description: Adds auto_fit map viewport config. Maps auto-center/zoom to visible tracks by default; set auto_fit: false to use fixed center and zoom.

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
Fixes an Obsidian reading-mode layout race where Leaflet maps could render only partially/gray until switching to edit mode. Maps now re-measure after preview layout settles and when their container becomes visible/resizes.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/CodyBontecou/isome-maps/compare/0.7.6...0.7.7

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/CodyBontecou/isome-maps/compare/0.7.2...0.7.3

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's new

- Added `show_stats` code-block option to show or hide the summary stats bar per map.
- Stats remain visible by default; set `show_stats: false` to hide them.
- Documented the new option in the README and usage reference.

## Example

```iso-me
source: exports/iso-export-2026-04.json
show_stats: false
```
releases.name: 0.7.2 — add show_stats map option

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's fixed

iso.me's new "all" markdown export wraps both visits and location points in a single `# iso.me Complete Export` file with table-formatted sections. The previous parser routed the whole file to the visits bullet-list parser and returned zero data, leaving the map empty with *"Export contains no visits or location points."*

- Markdown exports are now sliced into H1 sections so visits and location points are parsed independently from a combined file.
- Visits parser now handles the new table layout (`Arrived | Departed | Duration | Lat | Lon | Location | Address | Notes`) in addition to the legacy bullet-list format.
- Defensive try/catch around stats bar and dynamic-layer rendering so a single failure cannot blank the whole map.
- Inline duration legend removed; stats bar already covers that ground.

## Upgrading

If you saw "Export contains no visits or location points." with a recent iso.me export, this release fixes it — drop in the new files and reload the plugin.
releases.name: 0.7.1 — fix iso.me Complete Export parsing

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's new

Reference exports by date instead of full file paths — perfect for daily notes.

Three new settings:

- **Exports folder** — vault-relative folder holding your iso.me exports. Bare `source:` filenames in code blocks are looked up here.
- **Export filename pattern** — glob template with a `{date}` token (default `*{date}*`) used when resolving date keywords.
- **Export date format** — tokens `YYYY` / `MM` / `DD` (default `YYYY-MM-DD`).

`source:` now accepts:

- `today`
- `yesterday`
- A literal `YYYY-MM-DD` (e.g. `2026-05-01`)
- `last 7 days`, `last 30 days`, etc.
- `last week`

Drop `source: yesterday` in a daily-note template and the map renders the previous day's data automatically.

````markdown
```iso-me
source: yesterday
title: Where I went yesterday
```
````

With the defaults, this matches both iso.me's per-day filenames (`iso.me - Friday 2026-05-01 - all.json`) and the timestamped full export (`isome_complete_export_2026-05-01_121042.json`).
releases.name: 0.6.0 — exports folder & date keywords

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's new

Tap (or click) the route polyline to open a popup showing:

- **Path** — total distance walked along the route, summed over every recorded segment
- **Straight** — as-the-crow-flies distance between the start (green) and end (red) markers

Distances are formatted in meters under 1 km, otherwise km.
releases.name: 0.5.0 — tap route to see distance

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's new

Match iso.me's new **One file per day** export mode without hand-listing every file. `source:` (and items under `sources:`) now also accept:

- A folder path — every supported export inside is loaded:

  ```iso-me
  source: exports/april/
  ```

- A filename glob with `*` / `?` — matched files are loaded in alphabetical (chronological) order:

  ```iso-me
  source: exports/iso.me*.json
  ```

  ```iso-me
  sources:
    - exports/iso.me*visits.md
    - exports/iso.me*points.md
  ```

Wildcards apply to the filename component only; the directory part must be literal. Single-file (`source: path.json`) and explicit `sources:` lists keep working as before, so condensed exports and per-day exports both render the same way.
releases.name: 0.4.0 — folder & glob source expansion

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's new

- New `interactive: true` block flag adds a control bar above the map with a **day picker** (every unique day in the export plus *All days*) and a **time-of-day range slider**.
- Changing either control re-renders visits, routes, heatmap, and outliers in place and auto-fits the map bounds to the filtered data.
- Reset button restores *All days* and the full 24-hour window.

```iso-me
source: exports/iso-export-2026-04.json
interactive: true
height: 500
```

## Under the hood

- Layer renderers now write into a managed `L.LayerGroup` so the map can clear and redraw without being torn down.
releases.name: 0.3.0 — interactive day & time-of-day filters

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's new

- Small helper note under the tile provider dropdown clarifying that already-open notes keep their current basemap until the note (or Obsidian) is reloaded.

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's new

- Tile provider dropdown in plugin settings — pick from CartoDB Voyager/Positron/Dark Matter, OpenTopoMap, Esri World Imagery, OSM, or **Custom** (your own URL + attribution).
- Existing installs auto-migrate: if your saved tile URL matches a known preset, it's set to that provider; otherwise it falls through to Custom.

## Install

Update via Community Plugins, or drop `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/iso-me-maps/`.

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's new

### Combine multiple export files in one map (`sources:`)

iso.me's CSV and Markdown exports split visits and location points into separate files. A single `iso-me` block can now ingest several files at once and render them as one map:

````
```iso-me
sources:
  - exports/visits-2026-04.md
  - exports/points-2026-04.md
show_visits: true
show_routes: true
title: April 2026 trip
```
````

Inline forms also work: `sources: [a.md, b.md]` or `sources: a.md, b.md`. Visits and points from each file are concatenated and the map fits its bounds across everything.

`source:` (single file) still works exactly as before, and you can combine `source:` + `sources:` if you want — they're additive.

## Install

Drop `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/iso-me-maps/`, or install via [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat).

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's new

### CSV and Markdown export support
The plugin now accepts all three iso.me export formats — `.json`, `.csv`, and `.md`/`.markdown` — dispatched by file extension. Visits and points are auto-detected from the CSV header row or Markdown H1.

- **CSV visits**: header begins with `arrived_at` (with optional `duration_minutes`, `latitude`, `longitude`, `location_name`, `address`, `notes`).
- **CSV points**: header is `timestamp,timestamp_unix,latitude,longitude` (with optional `altitude`, `speed`, `horizontal_accuracy`, `is_outlier`).
- **Markdown visits**: `# iso.me Export` with `## <date>` day groups and `### <visit>` blocks of `- **Arrived/Departed/Duration/Address/Coordinates:** ...` bullets and `> notes`.
- **Markdown points**: `# iso.me Location Points Export` with `## <date>` day groups and `| Time | Lat | Lon | ... |` tables.

Markdown parsing assumes en-US-locale dates/times (the iso.me default). JSON and CSV are locale-independent.

### GPS glitch (outlier) rendering
The plugin now recognizes the `is_outlier` flag iso.me writes for points it has classified as GPS glitches.

- New `show_outliers:` block key (and global setting; default off) renders flagged points as small amber scatter dots.
- The route polyline and heatmap **always** exclude outliers regardless of the flag, so a single bad GPS spike won't bend the track.
- Outlier color is configurable in plugin settings.

## Install

Drop `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/iso-me-maps/`, or install via [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat).

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
**Bug fix:** Default tile provider switched from OpenStreetMap's volunteer servers to CartoDB Voyager.

OSM's volunteer servers reject requests from Obsidian desktop (Electron) because the referer header doesn't match their tile usage policy, returning HTTP 403 \"Access blocked.\" The new default uses CartoDB Voyager — OSM-styled, free for attribution, and accepts Electron requests.

Existing installs that saved the legacy OSM URL are migrated automatically on load. To revert manually, paste any other tile-server URL into Settings → iso.me Maps → Tile layer URL.
releases.name: 0.1.1 — fix desktop tile loading

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
First release of **iso.me Maps for Obsidian**.

Render interactive Leaflet maps inside notes from iso.me JSON exports.

## Features

- **Visit markers** — pins with popups (location name, address, arrived → departed, duration, notes)
- **Route polylines** — connected GPS tracks with green/red start/end markers; auto-downsamples >1500 points
- **Heatmap** — density overlay over the GPS points
- Auto-detects whether the export contains visits, location points, or both
- Configurable tile layer, colors, default zoom/height in plugin settings
- All resources bundled — no network needed beyond OSM tiles

## Usage

````markdown
```iso-me
source: exports/iso-export.json
height: 500
show_visits: true
show_routes: true
show_heatmap: false
```
````

`source` is required and resolved relative to the vault root. Generate the JSON via the iso.me iOS app (Settings → Export).

## Install via BRAT

1. Install the BRAT plugin in Obsidian.
2. BRAT → Add Beta Plugin → paste `CodyBontecou/obsidian-iso-me-maps`.
3. Enable "iso.me Maps" in Community Plugins.
releases.name: 0.1.0 — initial release
